---
title: "<span class='bold'>JP Bastos</span>  <span class='not-bold'>| Welcome</span>"
page-layout: full
---

<div style="text-align: center; margin-top: 30px; margin-bottom: 25px;">
  <img src="images/JP Headshot.png" alt="JP Bastos" style="width: 250px; border-radius: 50%;">
</div>

<div style="text-align: center; margin-top: 30px; margin-top: 20px;">
  <a href="../files/Job_Market_CV___JP_Bastos.pdf" class="btn btn-primary" role="button" download>Download my CV</a>
</div>

I recently completed my PhD in Agricultural & Applied Economics at Texas Tech University. I am currently a Research Assistant at the [Free Market Institute](https://www.depts.ttu.edu/freemarketinstitute/), and will be joining the [University of Austin (UATX)](https://www.uaustin.org/) as an Assistant Professor of Economics in the Center for Economics, Politics, and History in Fall 2026.

My [research](https://jpmvbastos.com/research.html) uses modern causal inference techniques to answer empirical questions on political economy and economic development, particularly in Latin America. At Texas Tech, I received the Davis College Outstanding Graduate Researcher Award and the Graduate School's Horn Distinguished Professors Graduate Achievement Award for Outstanding Research. 

My [Job Market Paper](https://jpmvbastos.com/files/research/Job%20Market%20Paper%20-%20JP%20Bastos.pdf) investigates the private returns to political office for Brazilian mayors, using a regression discontinuity design around close election races. It was awarded the [2026 Elinor and Vincent Ostrom Prize](https://www.publicchoicesociety.org/the-elinor-vincent-ostrom-prize/) for best paper by a graduate student at the Annual Meetings of the Public Choice Society.

I have worked as a policy analyst in the Executive and Legislative branches of City of Porto Alegre, Brazil, where I got to work on important economic reforms. I discuss policy issues as a regular contributor to [Exame](https://exame.com/autor/joao-pedro-bastos/), and my policy papers have been featured in some of the most popular media outlets in Brazil.

I also run the [Public Choice in Latin America](https://publicchoicelatinamerica.substack.com/) project, with Nicolás Cachanosky.

<div class="substack-embed" style="text-align: center; margin: 30px 0;">
  <iframe src="https://publicchoicelatinamerica.substack.com/embed" width="350" height="320" style="border:1px solid #EEE; background:white;" frameborder="0" scrolling="no"></iframe>
</div>
